[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"from pathlib import Path

from click.testing import CliRunner

from erk.cli.commands.pr import pr_group
from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import BranchDivergence
from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit
from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub
from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails, PullRequestInfo
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.fake import FakeGraphite
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.types import BranchMetadata
from tests.fakes.prompt_executor import FakePromptExecutor
from tests.test_utils.context_builders import build_workspace_test_context
from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_isolated_fs_env","new_string":"from pathlib import Path

from click.testing import CliRunner

from erk.cli.commands.pr import pr_group
from erk_shared.core.fakes import FakeLlmCaller
from erk_shared.core.llm_caller import LlmCallFailed, LlmResponse
from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import BranchDivergence
from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit
from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub
from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails, PullRequestInfo
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.fake import FakeGraphite
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.types import BranchMetadata
from tests.fakes.prompt_executor import FakePromptExecutor
from tests.test_utils.context_builders import build_workspace_test_context
from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_isolated_fs_env"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"from pathlib import Path

from click.testing import CliRunner

from erk.cli.commands.pr import pr_group
from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import BranchDivergence
from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit
from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub
from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails, PullRequestInfo
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.fake import FakeGraphite
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.types import BranchMetadata
from tests.fakes.prompt_executor import FakePromptExecutor
from tests.test_utils.context_builders import build_workspace_test_context
from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_isolated_fs_env","new_string":"from pathlib import Path

from click.testing import CliRunner

from erk.cli.commands.pr import pr_group
from erk_shared.core.fakes import FakeLlmCaller
from erk_shared.core.llm_caller import LlmCallFailed, LlmResponse
from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import BranchDivergence
from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit
from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeGitHub
from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PRDetails, PullRequestInfo
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.fake import FakeGraphite
from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.types import BranchMetadata
from tests.fakes.prompt_executor import FakePromptExecutor
from tests.test_utils.context_builders import build_workspace_test_context
from tests.test_utils.env_helpers import erk_isolated_fs_env"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"        github = FakeGitHub(
            authenticated=True,
            prs={"feature": pr_info},
            pr_details={123: pr_details},
            pr_bases={123: "main"},
        )
        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Add feature\n\nThis adds a new feature.",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
        )","new_string":"        github = FakeGitHub(
            authenticated=True,
            prs={"feature": pr_info},
            pr_details={123: pr_details},
            pr_bases={123: "main"},
        )
        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Add feature\n\nThis adds a new feature.",
        )
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text="Add feature\n\nThis adds a new feature."))

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
        )"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"        # Configure executor to fail on prompt
        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_error="Claude CLI execution failed",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
        )","new_string":"        # Configure executor to fail on prompt
        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_error="Claude CLI execution failed",
        )
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmCallFailed(message="Claude CLI execution failed"))

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
        )"}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        # Both URLs should be in output","new_string":"        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text="Title\n\nBody"))

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        # Both URLs should be in output"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
        )

        # Use --no-graphite to test standard flow output messaging
        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit", "--no-graphite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        # Should show "created" message for new PR","new_string":"        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text="Title\n\nBody"))

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
        )

        # Use --no-graphite to test standard flow output messaging
        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit", "--no-graphite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        # Should show "created" message for new PR"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"        executor = FakePromptExecutor(

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
        )

        # Use --no-graphite to test standard flow output messaging
        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit", "--no-graphite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        # Should show "found" message for existing PR","new_string":"        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text="Title\n\nBody"))

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
        )

        # Use --no-graphite to test standard flow output messaging
        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit", "--no-graphite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        # Should show "found" message for existing PR"}

[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"def test_pr_submit_shows_plan_context_phase() -> None:
    """Test that Phase 2 shows plan found for branches that have a PR.

    With PlannedPRBackend, any branch with a PR resolves to that PR as its plan.
    The submit command shows "Incorporating plan from issue #123" for the PR.
    """
    from datetime import UTC, datetime

    from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues
    from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueComment, IssueInfo

    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        pr_info = PullRequestInfo(
            number=123,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123",
            is_draft=False,
            title="Feature PR",
            checks_passing=True,
            owner="owner",
            repo="repo",
        )
        pr_details = PRDetails(
            number=123,
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123",
            title="Feature PR",
            body="",
            state="OPEN",
            is_draft=False,
            base_ref_name="main",
            head_ref_name="P5823-add-feature",
            is_cross_repository=False,
            mergeable="MERGEABLE",
            merge_state_status="CLEAN",
            owner="owner",
            repo="repo",
            labels=(),
        )

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            repository_roots={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", "P5823-add-feature"]},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.git_dir: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "P5823-add-feature"},
            commits_ahead={(env.cwd, "main"): 1},
            remote_urls={(env.git_dir, "origin"): "git@github.com:owner/repo.git"},
            diff_to_branch={(env.cwd, "main"): "diff --git a/file.py b/file.py\n+content"},
        )

        graphite = FakeGraphite(
            authenticated=True,
            branches={
                "P5823-add-feature": BranchMetadata(
                    name="P5823-add-feature",
                    parent="main",
                    children=[],
                    is_trunk=False,
                    commit_sha=None,
                ),
                "main": BranchMetadata(
                    name="main",
                    parent=None,
                    children=["P5823-add-feature"],
                    is_trunk=True,
                    commit_sha=None,
                ),
            },
        )

        # Create issue with plan metadata referencing comment (using proper format)
        now = datetime.now(UTC)
        issue_body = """<!-- erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->
<details>
<summary><code>plan-header</code></summary>

```yaml
schema_version: '2'
created_at: '2025-01-24T12:00:00Z'
created_by: testuser
plan_comment_id: 1000
```

</details>
<!-- /erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->"""
        plan_issue = IssueInfo(
            number=5823,
            title="[erk-plan] Add feature",
            body=issue_body,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5823",
            labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"],
            assignees=[],
            created_at=now,
            updated_at=now,
            author="testuser",
        )

        # Comment containing the plan content (using old format for backward compatibility)
        plan_body = (
            "<!-- erk:plan-content -->\n"
            "# Plan\n"
            "Add the feature implementation.\n"
            "<!-- /erk:plan-content -->"
        )
        plan_comment = IssueComment(
            id=1000,
            body=plan_body,
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5823#issuecomment-1000",
            author="testuser",
        )

        github_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(
            issues={5823: plan_issue},
            comments_with_urls={5823: [plan_comment]},
        )

        github = FakeGitHub(
            authenticated=True,
            prs={"P5823-add-feature": pr_info},
            pr_details={123: pr_details},
            pr_bases={123: "main"},
            issues_gateway=github_issues,
        )

        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            issues=github_issues,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit", "--no-graphite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        # Verify Phase 2 shows plan found (PR is the plan with PlannedPRBackend)
        assert "Phase 2: Getting diff and plan context" in result.output
        assert "Incorporating plan #123" in result.output","new_string":"def test_pr_submit_shows_plan_context_phase() -> None:
    """Test that Phase 2 shows plan found for branches that have a PR.

    With PlannedPRBackend, any branch with a PR resolves to that PR as its plan.
    The submit command shows "Incorporating plan from issue #123" for the PR.
    """
    from datetime import UTC, datetime

    from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues
    from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueComment, IssueInfo

    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        pr_info = PullRequestInfo(
            number=123,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123",
            is_draft=False,
            title="Feature PR",
            checks_passing=True,
            owner="owner",
            repo="repo",
        )
        pr_details = PRDetails(
            number=123,
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123",
            title="Feature PR",
            body="",
            state="OPEN",
            is_draft=False,
            base_ref_name="main",
            head_ref_name="P5823-add-feature",
            is_cross_repository=False,
            mergeable="MERGEABLE",
            merge_state_status="CLEAN",
            owner="owner",
            repo="repo",
            labels=(),
        )

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            repository_roots={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", "P5823-add-feature"]},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.git_dir: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "P5823-add-feature"},
            commits_ahead={(env.cwd, "main"): 1},
            remote_urls={(env.git_dir, "origin"): "git@github.com:owner/repo.git"},
            diff_to_branch={(env.cwd, "main"): "diff --git a/file.py b/file.py\n+content"},
        )

        graphite = FakeGraphite(
            authenticated=True,
            branches={
                "P5823-add-feature": BranchMetadata(
                    name="P5823-add-feature",
                    parent="main",
                    children=[],
                    is_trunk=False,
                    commit_sha=None,
                ),
                "main": BranchMetadata(
                    name="main",
                    parent=None,
                    children=["P5823-add-feature"],
                    is_trunk=True,
                    commit_sha=None,
                ),
            },
        )

        # Create issue with plan metadata referencing comment (using proper format)
        now = datetime.now(UTC)
        issue_body = """<!-- erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->
<details>
<summary><code>plan-header</code></summary>

```yaml
schema_version: '2'
created_at: '2025-01-24T12:00:00Z'
created_by: testuser
plan_comment_id: 1000
```

</details>
<!-- /erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->"""
        plan_issue = IssueInfo(
            number=5823,
            title="[erk-plan] Add feature",
            body=issue_body,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5823",
            labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"],
            assignees=[],
            created_at=now,
            updated_at=now,
            author="testuser",
        )

        # Comment containing the plan content (using old format for backward compatibility)
        plan_body = (
            "<!-- erk:plan-content -->\n"
            "# Plan\n"
            "Add the feature implementation.\n"
            "<!-- /erk:plan-content -->"
        )
        plan_comment = IssueComment(
            id=1000,
            body=plan_body,
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5823#issuecomment-1000",
            author="testuser",
        )

        github_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(
            issues={5823: plan_issue},
            comments_with_urls={5823: [plan_comment]},
        )

        github = FakeGitHub(
            authenticated=True,
            prs={"P5823-add-feature": pr_info},
            pr_details={123: pr_details},
            pr_bases={123: "main"},
            issues_gateway=github_issues,
        )

        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text="Title\n\nBody"))

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
            issues=github_issues,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit", "--no-graphite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        # Verify Phase 2 shows plan found (PR is the plan with PlannedPRBackend)
        assert "Phase 2: Getting diff and plan context" in result.output
        assert "Incorporating plan #123" in result.output"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"def test_pr_submit_shows_plan_context_with_objective() -> None:
    """Test that Phase 2 shows plan found for branches that have a PR.

    With PlannedPRBackend, any branch with a PR resolves to that PR as its plan,
    even when the old issue had an objective linkage.
    """
    from datetime import UTC, datetime

    from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues
    from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueComment, IssueInfo

    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        pr_info = PullRequestInfo(
            number=123,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123",
            is_draft=False,
            title="Feature PR",
            checks_passing=True,
            owner="owner",
            repo="repo",
        )
        pr_details = PRDetails(
            number=123,
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123",
            title="Feature PR",
            body="",
            state="OPEN",
            is_draft=False,
            base_ref_name="main",
            head_ref_name="P5823-add-feature",
            is_cross_repository=False,
            mergeable="MERGEABLE",
            merge_state_status="CLEAN",
            owner="owner",
            repo="repo",
            labels=(),
        )

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            repository_roots={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", "P5823-add-feature"]},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.git_dir: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "P5823-add-feature"},
            commits_ahead={(env.cwd, "main"): 1},
            remote_urls={(env.git_dir, "origin"): "git@github.com:owner/repo.git"},
            diff_to_branch={(env.cwd, "main"): "diff --git a/file.py b/file.py\n+content"},
        )

        graphite = FakeGraphite(
            authenticated=True,
            branches={
                "P5823-add-feature": BranchMetadata(
                    name="P5823-add-feature",
                    parent="main",
                    children=[],
                    is_trunk=False,
                    commit_sha=None,
                ),
                "main": BranchMetadata(
                    name="main",
                    parent=None,
                    children=["P5823-add-feature"],
                    is_trunk=True,
                    commit_sha=None,
                ),
            },
        )

        # Create issue with plan metadata referencing comment AND objective
        now = datetime.now(UTC)
        issue_body = """<!-- erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->
<details>
<summary><code>plan-header</code></summary>

```yaml
schema_version: '2'
created_at: '2025-01-24T12:00:00Z'
created_by: testuser
plan_comment_id: 1000
objective_issue: 5000
```

</details>
<!-- /erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->"""
        plan_issue = IssueInfo(
            number=5823,
            title="[erk-plan] Add feature",
            body=issue_body,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5823",
            labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"],
            assignees=[],
            created_at=now,
            updated_at=now,
            author="testuser",
        )

        # Objective issue
        objective_issue = IssueInfo(
            number=5000,
            title="Improve PR workflow",
            body="Objective body",
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5000",
            labels=["erk-objective"],
            assignees=[],
            created_at=now,
            updated_at=now,
            author="testuser",
        )

        # Comment containing the plan content (using old format for backward compatibility)
        plan_body = (
            "<!-- erk:plan-content -->\n"
            "# Plan\n"
            "Add the feature implementation.\n"
            "<!-- /erk:plan-content -->"
        )
        plan_comment = IssueComment(
            id=1000,
            body=plan_body,
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5823#issuecomment-1000",
            author="testuser",
        )

        github_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(
            issues={5823: plan_issue, 5000: objective_issue},
            comments_with_urls={5823: [plan_comment]},
        )

        github = FakeGitHub(
            authenticated=True,
            prs={"P5823-add-feature": pr_info},
            pr_details={123: pr_details},
            pr_bases={123: "main"},
            issues_gateway=github_issues,
        )

        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            issues=github_issues,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit", "--no-graphite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        # Verify Phase 2 shows plan found (PR is the plan with PlannedPRBackend)
        assert "Phase 2: Getting diff and plan context" in result.output
        assert "Incorporating plan #123" in result.output","new_string":"def test_pr_submit_shows_plan_context_with_objective() -> None:
    """Test that Phase 2 shows plan found for branches that have a PR.

    With PlannedPRBackend, any branch with a PR resolves to that PR as its plan,
    even when the old issue had an objective linkage.
    """
    from datetime import UTC, datetime

    from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.fake import FakeGitHubIssues
    from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueComment, IssueInfo

    runner = CliRunner()
    with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env:
        pr_info = PullRequestInfo(
            number=123,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123",
            is_draft=False,
            title="Feature PR",
            checks_passing=True,
            owner="owner",
            repo="repo",
        )
        pr_details = PRDetails(
            number=123,
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123",
            title="Feature PR",
            body="",
            state="OPEN",
            is_draft=False,
            base_ref_name="main",
            head_ref_name="P5823-add-feature",
            is_cross_repository=False,
            mergeable="MERGEABLE",
            merge_state_status="CLEAN",
            owner="owner",
            repo="repo",
            labels=(),
        )

        git = FakeGit(
            git_common_dirs={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            repository_roots={env.cwd: env.git_dir},
            local_branches={env.cwd: ["main", "P5823-add-feature"]},
            default_branches={env.cwd: "main"},
            trunk_branches={env.git_dir: "main"},
            current_branches={env.cwd: "P5823-add-feature"},
            commits_ahead={(env.cwd, "main"): 1},
            remote_urls={(env.git_dir, "origin"): "git@github.com:owner/repo.git"},
            diff_to_branch={(env.cwd, "main"): "diff --git a/file.py b/file.py\n+content"},
        )

        graphite = FakeGraphite(
            authenticated=True,
            branches={
                "P5823-add-feature": BranchMetadata(
                    name="P5823-add-feature",
                    parent="main",
                    children=[],
                    is_trunk=False,
                    commit_sha=None,
                ),
                "main": BranchMetadata(
                    name="main",
                    parent=None,
                    children=["P5823-add-feature"],
                    is_trunk=True,
                    commit_sha=None,
                ),
            },
        )

        # Create issue with plan metadata referencing comment AND objective
        now = datetime.now(UTC)
        issue_body = """<!-- erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->
<details>
<summary><code>plan-header</code></summary>

```yaml
schema_version: '2'
created_at: '2025-01-24T12:00:00Z'
created_by: testuser
plan_comment_id: 1000
objective_issue: 5000
```

</details>
<!-- /erk:metadata-block:plan-header -->"""
        plan_issue = IssueInfo(
            number=5823,
            title="[erk-plan] Add feature",
            body=issue_body,
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5823",
            labels=["erk-pr", "erk-plan"],
            assignees=[],
            created_at=now,
            updated_at=now,
            author="testuser",
        )

        # Objective issue
        objective_issue = IssueInfo(
            number=5000,
            title="Improve PR workflow",
            body="Objective body",
            state="OPEN",
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5000",
            labels=["erk-objective"],
            assignees=[],
            created_at=now,
            updated_at=now,
            author="testuser",
        )

        # Comment containing the plan content (using old format for backward compatibility)
        plan_body = (
            "<!-- erk:plan-content -->\n"
            "# Plan\n"
            "Add the feature implementation.\n"
            "<!-- /erk:plan-content -->"
        )
        plan_comment = IssueComment(
            id=1000,
            body=plan_body,
            url="https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/5823#issuecomment-1000",
            author="testuser",
        )

        github_issues = FakeGitHubIssues(
            issues={5823: plan_issue, 5000: objective_issue},
            comments_with_urls={5823: [plan_comment]},
        )

        github = FakeGitHub(
            authenticated=True,
            prs={"P5823-add-feature": pr_info},
            pr_details={123: pr_details},
            pr_bases={123: "main"},
            issues_gateway=github_issues,
        )

        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text="Title\n\nBody"))

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
            issues=github_issues,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit", "--no-graphite"], obj=ctx)

        assert result.exit_code == 0
        # Verify Phase 2 shows plan found (PR is the plan with PlannedPRBackend)
        assert "Phase 2: Getting diff and plan context" in result.output
        assert "Incorporating plan #123" in result.output"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            issues=FakeGitHubIssues(),  # No plan for "feature" branch (empty issues)
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit"], obj=ctx)","new_string":"        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text="Title\n\nBody"))

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
            issues=FakeGitHubIssues(),  # No plan for "feature" branch (empty issues)
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit"], obj=ctx)"}
[Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","old_string":"        github = FakeGitHub(
            authenticated=True,
            prs={"feature": pr_info},
            pr_details={123: pr_details},
            pr_bases={123: "main"},
        )

        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit", "--force"], obj=ctx)","new_string":"        github = FakeGitHub(
            authenticated=True,
            prs={"feature": pr_info},
            pr_details={123: pr_details},
            pr_bases={123: "main"},
        )

        executor = FakePromptExecutor(
            available=True,
            simulated_prompt_output="Title\n\nBody",
        )
        llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text="Title\n\nBody"))

        ctx = build_workspace_test_context(
            env,
            git=git,
            github=github,
            graphite=graphite,
            prompt_executor=executor,
            llm_caller=llm_caller,
        )

        result = runner.invoke(pr_group, ["submit", "--force"], obj=ctx)"}

... [28 lines omitted] ...

[Tool: Bash] {"command":"grep -c "simulated_prompt_error" /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","description":"Count simulated_prompt_error occurrences"}
[Tool: Bash] {"command":"grep -c "simulated_prompt_error" /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","description":"Count simulated_prompt_error occurrences"}
[Tool: Bash] {"command":"grep -A 5 "simulated_prompt_error" /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","description":"Verify the error case was handled correctly"}
[Tool: Bash] {"command":"grep -A 5 "simulated_prompt_error" /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py","description":"Verify the error case was handled correctly"}
[Tool: Bash] {"command":"awk '/test_pr_submit_fails_when_commit_message_generation_fails/,/^def test_/ {print NR": "$0}' /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py | grep -A 10 "llm_caller"","description":"Check the error test case configuration"}
[Tool: Bash] {"command":"awk '/test_pr_submit_fails_when_commit_message_generation_fails/,/^def test_/ {print NR": "$0}' /home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py | grep -A 10 "llm_caller"","description":"Check the error test case configuration"}
session.user:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below: Analysis: Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:
1. The user triggered `/erk:plan-implement 8801` which is a command to implement a plan from GitHub issue #8801.
2. The plan is about replacing Claude CLI subprocess with direct Anthropic API calls for PR description generation. The key changes are: - Extend `LlmCaller` ABC with `max_tokens` parameter - Update `AnthropicLlmCaller` to accept `max_tokens` - Update `FakeLlmCaller` with `max_tokens` and call recording - Update all existing callers with `max_tokens=50` - Switch `CommitMessageGenerator` from `PromptExecutor` to `LlmCaller` - Update construction sites - Update tests - Remove dead code
3. I set up the implementation, read the plan, created todos, and started implementing phase by phase.
4. Key files modified: - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/llm_caller.py` - Added `max_tokens: int` param to ABC - `src/erk/core/fast_llm.py` - Pass through `max_tokens` instead of hardcoded 50 - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/fakes.py` - Rewrote `FakeLlmCaller` from frozen dataclass to mutable class with call recording - `src/erk/core/commit_message_generator.py` - Complete rewrite to use `LlmCaller` instead of `PromptExecutor` - `src/erk/cli/commands/objective/plan_cmd.py` - Added `max_tokens=50` - `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_dispatch.py` - Added `max_tokens=50` - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` - Changed constructor call - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rewrite_cmd.py` - Changed constructor call - `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_pr_description.py` - Changed constructor call - `tests/core/test_commit_message_generator.py` - Complete rewrite to use `FakeLlmCaller` - `tests/core/test_fast_llm.py` - Added `max_tokens=50` to calls
5. Errors encountered: - Ruff lint errors: Line too long in `fakes.py` and `one_shot_dispatch.py` - fixed by breaking lines - Ruff format issues on 3 files - fixed by running `ruff format` - Integration test failures: `test_pr_rewrite_happy_path` and others failed because tests were setting up `FakePromptExecutor` with `simulated_prompt_output` but `CommitMessageGenerator` now uses `ctx.llm_caller` which defaults to returning "test-slug". Fixed by adding `llm_caller=FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text=...))` to all affected test contexts.
6. Four test files needed fixing for the integration test failures: - `tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py` - Fixed manually - `tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py` - Fixed via background agent (add398d) - `tests/commands/pr/test_submit_graphite_disabled.py` - Fixed via background agent (a87e3fc) - completed - `tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_update_pr_description.py` - Fixed via background agent (a61b69a) - completed
7. The background agent for test_submit.py (add398d) was the last to complete - its output shows it successfully updated all 15+ occurrences and ran ruff format and ruff check.
8. Current state: All three background agents have completed. The test_submit.py agent finished making changes. Still need to: - Run full CI checks to verify everything passes - Clean up impl context - Submit PR
9. User messages were system reminders about TodoWrite tool - no direct user feedback or corrections.
Let me also track the pending steps from the plan-implement workflow: - Step 8: Final Verification - not done - Step 9: Signal GitHub Ended - not done - Step 10: Upload Session - not done - Step 11: Clean Up Impl Context - not done - Step 12: Run CI Iteratively - partially done (need final run) - Step 13: Submit PR - not done
Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user triggered `/erk:plan-implement 8801` to implement GitHub plan #8801: "Replace Claude CLI subprocess with direct Anthropic API call for PR description generation." The goal is to make PR description generation ~5x faster by using direct Anthropic SDK calls (`AnthropicLlmCaller`) instead of spawning a `claude --print --model haiku` subprocess via `PromptExecutor`. The implementation follows the erk plan-implement workflow (setup → read plan → implement → CI → submit PR).
2. Key Technical Concepts: - `LlmCaller` ABC pattern with discriminated union return types (`LlmResponse | NoApiKey | LlmCallFailed`) - `AnthropicLlmCaller` - concrete implementation using Anthropic SDK directly - `FakeLlmCaller` - test double with call recording via `LlmCall` NamedTuple - `CommitMessageGenerator` - generator-based pattern yielding `ProgressEvent | CompletionEvent` - `PromptExecutor` - the old subprocess-based approach being replaced - `ErkContext` dependency injection (`ctx.llm_caller`, `ctx.prompt_executor`) - `build_workspace_test_context` for integration test setup with `**kwargs` passthrough - Background task agents for parallel test file updates
3. Files and Code Sections:
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/llm_caller.py`** — ABC interface for LLM calls. Added `max_tokens: int` parameter. ```python class LlmCaller(ABC): @abstractmethod def call( self, prompt: str, *, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int ) -> LlmResponse | NoApiKey | LlmCallFailed: """Execute an LLM call.""" ... ```
- **`src/erk/core/fast_llm.py`** — Real Anthropic SDK implementation. Changed hardcoded `max_tokens=50` to pass-through parameter. ```python def call( self, prompt: str, *, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int ) -> LlmResponse | NoApiKey | LlmCallFailed: # ... now uses max_tokens=max_tokens in client.messages.create() ```
- **`packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/core/fakes.py`** — Changed `FakeLlmCaller` from frozen dataclass to mutable class with call recording. ```python class LlmCall(NamedTuple): prompt: str system_prompt: str max_tokens: int
class FakeLlmCaller(LlmCaller): def __init__( self, *, response: LlmResponse | NoApiKey | LlmCallFailed, responses: list[LlmResponse | NoApiKey | LlmCallFailed] | None = None, ) -> None: self.response = response self._responses = list(responses) if responses is not None else None self._response_index = 0 self.calls: list[LlmCall] = []
def call( self, prompt: str, *, system_prompt: str, max_tokens: int ) -> LlmResponse | NoApiKey | LlmCallFailed: self.calls.append( LlmCall(prompt=prompt, system_prompt=system_prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens) ) # returns from responses queue or falls back to self.response ```
- **`src/erk/core/commit_message_generator.py`** — Complete rewrite. Removed `PromptExecutor`, threading, `Time`, `model`, `USE_SYSTEM_PROMPT_REPLACEMENT`, and `_build_prompt()`. Now uses `LlmCaller` directly. ```python class CommitMessageGenerator: def __init__(self, llm_caller: LlmCaller) -> None: self._llm_caller = llm_caller
def generate(self, request: CommitMessageRequest) -> Generator[...]: # ... builds prompt, calls self._llm_caller.call(user_prompt, system_prompt=..., max_tokens=4096) # Maps LlmResponse → success, NoApiKey/LlmCallFailed → error ```
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:655`** — Construction site update: ```python msg_gen = CommitMessageGenerator(ctx.llm_caller) # was: CommitMessageGenerator(ctx.prompt_executor, time=ctx.time, model="haiku") ```
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rewrite_cmd.py:125`** — Same construction site update.
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/update_pr_description.py:120`** — Same construction site update.
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/objective/plan_cmd.py:60`** — Added `max_tokens=50` to existing caller.
- **`src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_dispatch.py:208`** — Added `max_tokens=50` to existing caller (also fixed line length).
- **`tests/core/test_commit_message_generator.py`** — Complete rewrite from `FakePromptExecutor` to `FakeLlmCaller`. Tests now assert on `caller.calls[0].prompt`, `caller.calls[0].system_prompt`, `caller.calls[0].max_tokens` instead of `executor.prompt_calls`. Removed `test_generate_uses_custom_model` test (model is no longer a parameter).
- **`tests/core/test_fast_llm.py`** — Added `max_tokens=50` to all `.call()` invocations.
- **`tests/commands/pr/test_rewrite.py`** — Updated manually. Changed `_make_rewrite_fakes` to return 5-tuple including `FakeLlmCaller`. Updated all 7 callers to unpack and pass `llm_caller=llm_caller` to `build_workspace_test_context`.
- **`tests/commands/pr/test_submit.py`** — Updated via background agent (add398d). Added imports for `FakeLlmCaller`/`LlmResponse`/`LlmCallFailed`. Added `llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text=...))` after each `FakePromptExecutor` with `simulated_prompt_output`, and `llm_caller = FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmCallFailed(message=...))` for error cases. 17 total additions. Agent ran ruff format and ruff check.
- **`tests/commands/pr/test_submit_graphite_disabled.py`** — Updated via background agent (a87e3fc). Added imports and `llm_caller` for 2 tests with `simulated_prompt_output`. Confirmed passing ruff format.
- **`tests/unit/cli/commands/exec/scripts/test_update_pr_description.py`** — Updated via background agent (a61b69a). Updated `_make_standard_fakes` helper to return 5-tuple including `FakeLlmCaller`. Updated all 6 callers. Ran ruff format.
4. Errors and Fixes: - **Ruff E501 line too long** in `fakes.py` (line 345) and `one_shot_dispatch.py` (line 208): Fixed by breaking long lines across multiple lines. - **Ruff format failures** on 3 files (`fakes.py`, `commit_message_generator.py`, `test_commit_message_generator.py`): Fixed by running `uv run ruff format` on them. - **Integration test failure `test_pr_rewrite_happy_path`**: Tests set up `FakePromptExecutor(simulated_prompt_output=...)` but `CommitMessageGenerator` now reads from `ctx.llm_caller` which defaulted to `FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text="test-slug"))`. Fixed by passing `llm_caller=FakeLlmCaller(response=LlmResponse(text=<same_text>))` to `build_workspace_test_context` in all affected integration tests across 4 test files. - **Pre-existing unrelated failure** `test_execute_split_plan_creates_worktrees` (FakeGit missing `worktree` attribute): Not our bug, ignored.
5. Problem Solving: - Core architecture problem: Replacing subprocess-based `PromptExecutor` with direct API `LlmCaller` for PR descriptions while preserving the generator-based progress event pattern. - Testing problem: The `FakeLlmCaller` was a frozen dataclass that couldn't track calls. Solved by converting to a regular class with `calls: list[LlmCall]` for test assertions. - Integration test cascade: Changing `CommitMessageGenerator`'s constructor signature broke integration tests in 4 files (~40 test functions). Solved by parallelizing fixes across 3 background agents + 1 manual fix.
6. All User Messages: - Message 1: `/erk:plan-implement 8801` command trigger with full implementation workflow instructions - Messages 2-4: System reminders about TodoWrite tool usage (automated, not user feedback) - No direct user feedback or corrections were given.
7. Pending Tasks: - Run final CI checks to verify ALL tests pass across the entire test suite - Format check on all modified files (agents may have left formatting issues) - Signal GitHub ended (`erk exec impl-signal ended`) - Upload session (`erk exec upload-impl-session`) - Clean up impl context (`erk exec cleanup-impl-context`) - Submit PR (`erk pr submit`) - Run `erk pr check --stage=impl`
8. Current Work: Three background agents were completing integration test fixes: - Agent `add398d` (test_submit.py) — just completed, made 17 `FakeLlmCaller` additions, ran ruff format/check - Agent `a87e3fc` (test_submit_graphite_disabled.py) — completed successfully - Agent `a61b69a` (test_update_pr_description.py) — completed successfully
All three agents have now finished. The last `TaskOutput` call for `add398d` returned with status `running` but the output showed it had completed all edits and was running final verification (ruff check). The full output is in `/tmp/claude/tasks/add398d.output`.
9. Optional Next Step: Run the full CI test suite to verify all changes pass, then proceed through the remaining plan-implement workflow steps (signal ended, upload session, cleanup impl context, submit PR). Specifically:
```bash uv run pytest tests/ -x --timeout=30 -q --ignore=tests/cli/commands/split 2>&1 | tail -30 ```
Then format-check all modified test files, especially `test_submit.py` which the agent reformatted. Then proceed to Steps 9-13 of the plan-implement workflow.. Please continue the conversation from where we left it off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.
